rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative difluoroethylation/cyclization of alkenyl carboxylic acids, alcohols, and amides with sodium difluoroethylsulfinate (NaSO2CF2Me) to give difluoroethylated lactones, dihydroisobenzofurans, and iminoisobenzofurans.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the difluoroethylsulfinate anion (MeCF2SO2-) generates MeCF2 radical via loss of SO2; subsequent radical adduct is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation that is trapped by the intramolecular nucleophile; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: MeCF2 radical (1,1-difluoroethyl radical) from anodic oxidation of MeCF2SO2- and loss of SO2
Benzylic C-radical intermediate A from MeCF2 addition to alkene
Benzylic carbocation intermediate B from anodic oxidation of A

electron_flow: Anode first oxidizes MeCF2SO2- (lower oxidation potential than substrate, confirmed by CV) to release SO2 and generate MeCF2 radical, which adds to the alkene to give benzylic radical A; A is oxidized again at the anode to carbocation B, which undergoes intramolecular nucleophilic cyclization by the pendant CO2H/OH/CONHPh group, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited, supporting a free radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a decreased to 13%; radical trapping adduct 12a isolated, confirming MeCF2 radical.

probe_reagents.name: Difluoroethyl sulfone (alternative MeCF2 source)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeded poorly; sulfone reduction not compatible with the electrochemically driven CF2Me radical cyclization.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2, and 1a+2
probe_reagents.observation: NaSO2CF2Me (2) oxidized at ~0.70 V vs. 1.3 V for substrate 1a, indicating sulfinate is oxidized first; current increase in 1:1 mixture; controlled-potential electrolysis at 0.70 V sufficient to drive reaction.

notes: Method also extends to cyclopropyldifluoromethylation using sodium cyclopropyldifluoromethylsulfinate (75–80% for benzoxazines). Sulfone-based MeCF2 precursor is ineffective, suggesting the sulfinate-specific anodic oxidation is essential. No KIE or DFT data reported.
